sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
भूय एव महाबाहो श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।
यत्तेऽहं प्रियमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śṛṇu me paramaṁ vacaḥ
yat te ’haṁ priyamāṇāya vakṣyāmi hita-kāmyayā
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: 'Again, O mighty-armed one, hear My supreme word, which I shall speak to you who are dear to Me, desiring your welfare.'
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals a deeper layer of self-disclosure, inviting Arjuna to listen once more—not for information, but for intimate recognition. The verse frames the next teachings as relational medicine: knowledge offered because the student is loved and the teacher desires his psychological wholeness.
psychological_state: Secure-base activation: the student is re-grounded in relational safety before confronting vaster aspects of the teacher’s identity, preventing overwhelm or dissociation.
trigger_scenario: A therapist says, 'Let’s go back over what we’ve touched on, but this time I’ll share more of why I really care about where you’re heading.'
anchor_text: Listen once more, beloved; these words are for your healing.
